pattern: The pass sequence is a deterministic walk around a fixed-size circle, so simulating it with a visited set until someone repeats directly answers who never got the ball.
hints: Model the n friends as positions 0..n-1 and simulate exactly what the rules describe: on turn i the ball moves i*k steps clockwise from wherever it currently is.
The simulation always ends, because eventually some position must be visited twice; stop the instant that happens.
The losers are every position from 0..n-1 that the simulation never visited, reported back as 1-indexed friend numbers in ascending order.

edge_inputs.description: a single friend, who trivially gets the ball
maximum n with k just under n
k equal to n loops back to the start immediately
every friend eventually touches the ball, so losers is empty
moderate n with k sharing a factor with n

stress.description: random n and k across the full legal range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 50)
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [n, k]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def circularGameLosers(self, n, k):
        visited = [False] * n
        current = 0
        visited[0] = True
        turn = 1
        while True:
            nxt = (current + turn * k) % n
            if visited[nxt]:
                break
            visited[nxt] = True
            current = nxt
            turn += 1
        return [i + 1 for i in range(n) if not visited[i]]
